compiled_state.title: Full test suite triggered by high-centrality files records as failed with no specific failing tests
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: When record-test-status.sh runs the full test suite (triggered by high-centrality files like plugin.json), the suite sometimes records as failed with an empty failed_tests field. This appears to be a timeout issue — the full suite of ~500 tests exceeds the tool timeout ceiling. The test gate then blocks commits even though no specific test failed. This blocked the version bump commit for eb00-efd0.

comments.body: Known issue match: INC-001 (Tool Timeout Ceiling exit 144) and INC-003 (Broad Test Commands Killed by Timeout). Both reference test-batched.sh as the solution for long test suites.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 4 (INTERMEDIATE). User requested ESCALATED investigation tier. severity=2 (blocks commits), complexity=2 (multi-file interaction: record-test-status.sh + pre-commit-test-gate.sh + tool timeout + centrality + RED zone), environment=0, intermittent=0.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned (high confidence). The code explicitly distinguishes timeout (exit 144) from failed. Empty failed_tests for full-suite is intentional (REVIEW-DEFENSE comment). But timeouts are being miscoded as failed — contradicts documented design. test-batched.sh is the documented solution (CLAUDE.md rule 12, INC-001, INC-003).
comments.author: Test
